name: Public Sector Master Value Pack
description: Comprehensive reasoning foundation for B2G and public sector technology sales, solution architecture, and value engineering. Covers Federal Government, State & Local Government, Education & Public Institutions, Public Health & Human Services, and Public Infrastructure & Utilities plus cross-cutting technology and governance themes.

subSegments: Justice / Law Enforcement (DOJ, FBI, DEA, BOP)
taxonomies.description: Federal executive branch departments, agencies, and independent commissions. $6.8T+ annual federal spending.
50 states, 3,100+ counties, 19,000+ municipalities. $3.5T+ combined annual spending.
taxonomies.geographicConcentration: Nationwide, state capitals, county seats, all communities

taxonomies.description: Public education from K-12 through higher ed, plus workforce development and libraries. $800B+ annual spending.

taxonomies.segment: D. Public Health and Human Services
taxonomies.description: Health and human services delivery including Medicaid, public health, nutrition, housing, and workforce support. $1T+ program spending.
Physical infrastructure and utility services. $400B+ annual capital and operating spending.

taxonomies.segment: F. Cross-Cutting: Public Sector Technology and Governance
taxonomies.description: Technology and governance capabilities that cut across all public sector segments.

pains.name: Legacy System Maintenance Burden
pains.description: Aging COBOL/mainframe, on-premise, and end-of-life systems consume disproportionate O&M budgets, limit feature agility, and create workforce/skills pipeline risk.
symptoms: Vendor support contracts at premium pricing
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services

pains.name: Cybersecurity Vulnerability & Threat Exposure
pains.description: Public sector agencies face nation-state, ransomware, and insider threats with under-resourced SOCs, inadequate Zero Trust implementation, and slow patching cycles.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services
sources: IBM Cost of Data Breach Report 2024

pains.name: Case / Claims Backlog Accumulation
pains.description: Human services, benefits, courts, and procurement offices accumulate backlogs due to manual processing, insufficient staffing, and workflow bottlenecks.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services
sources: SSA Office of the Inspector General

pains.name: Fraud, Waste, and Abuse in Benefit Programs
pains.description: Improper payment rates in Medicaid, UI, SNAP, and other programs range from 5-20%. Identity theft and organized fraud rings exploit weak verification.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services

pains.name: Grant Management & Compliance Complexity
pains.description: 2,200+ federal grant programs with fragmented systems create subrecipient monitoring gaps, late reporting, and single audit findings.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services

pains.name: Workforce Shortage & Skills Gaps
pains.description: Public sector faces STEM, cybersecurity, clinical, and IT skills shortages with non-competitive pay, slow hiring (6-18 months), and aging workforce.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services
sources: Partnership for Public Service Best Places to Work

pains.name: Procurement Cycle Time & Inefficiency
pains.description: Federal and state procurement cycles average 6-18 months, delaying mission-critical acquisitions.

pains.name: Data Silos & Interoperability Failures
pains.description: Agency stovepipes prevent holistic citizen/service views. Systems don't share data across programs causing duplication and waste.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services

pains.name: Citizen / Constituent Service Friction
pains.description: Citizens face long wait times, multiple touchpoints, and opaque processes when accessing government services.

pains.name: Infrastructure Asset Degradation & Deferred Maintenance
pains.description: Roads, bridges, water systems, and transit assets accumulate deferred maintenance backlogs exceeding $1T nationally.
symptoms: Safety incidents related to asset condition

pains.name: Regulatory Compliance & Audit Burden
pains.description: Multiple overlapping regulatory frameworks create compliance overhead. Audit findings repeat year after year.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services

pains.name: Budget Volatility & Revenue Uncertainty
pains.description: Counter-cyclical demands rise when revenues fall. Federal shutdowns and state balanced-budget requirements force abrupt cuts.

pains.name: Emergency Preparedness & Response Gaps
pains.description: Natural disasters, pandemics, and cyber events expose gaps in coordination, communication, and recovery.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services

pains.name: FOIA / Public Records Request Backlogs
pains.description: Federal and state open records requests accumulate with manual search processes and litigation hold complexities.

pains.name: Student Enrollment Decline & Fiscal Stress
pains.description: K-12 enrollment declining in many districts; higher ed faces enrollment cliffs, reduced state support, and tuition dependency.
sources: SHEF State Higher Ed Finance FY2023

pains.name: Medicaid Administrative Complexity & State Share Pressure
pains.description: Medicaid is states' largest budget item. Eligibility determination and federal compliance create massive administrative burden.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services

pains.name: Transit Ridership Decline & Revenue Recovery
pains.description: Post-pandemic ridership recovery is incomplete. Fare revenue shortfalls strain operating budgets.

pains.name: Water / Wastewater System Affordability & Infrastructure Gap
pains.description: Lead service lines, PFAS contamination, and aging pipes require $1T+ investment. Rate increases face affordability constraints.
symptoms: Lead service line replacement backlog >20 years
sources: EPA Drinking Water Infrastructure Needs Survey 7th Assessment
AWWA 2023 State of the Water Industry

pains.name: Criminal Justice Case Processing Delays
pains.description: Pre-trial detention lengths and court backlogs strain jail capacity, violate speedy trial rights, and increase costs.

pains.name: Federal Acquisition Workforce Shortage & Skill Gaps
pains.description: Federal 1102 series faces 20%+ vacancy rates. Complex IT procurements require specialized skills.

pains.name: Program Eligibility Determination Errors & Delays
pains.description: Means-tested programs face eligibility determination delays that deny timely benefits and create churn.
affectedSegments: D. Public Health and Human Services
sources: KFF Medicaid Enrollment and Unwinding Tracker

pains.name: School Safety & Security Incident Response
pains.description: K-12 and higher ed face physical security threats and cybersecurity incidents with fragmented preparedness.
symptoms: Mental health referral wait times long
sources: GSSA State of School Safety Report

pains.name: Cloud Migration Complexity & Cost Overrun
pains.description: Cloud adoption faces migration cost overruns (30-50% above estimates), data egress costs, and FedRAMP ATO delays.
sources: MeriTalk Cloudy with a Chance of Insight 2023

pains.name: Broadband / Digital Equity Access Gap
pains.description: 7% of Americans lack broadband access. BEAD and digital equity programs require complex compliance and deployment tracking.
sources: Pew Research Center Internet/Broadband Fact Sheet

pains.name: Public Pension & OPEB Liability Pressure
pains.description: State and local pension systems face $1T+ unfunded liabilities. ARC payments crowd out current services.
sources: Pew Charitable Trusts State Pension Funding Gap 2023

kpis.name: Legacy System O&M Ratio
kpis.formula: Legacy O&M Spend / Total IT Spend * 100%

kpis.name: Modernization Spend Ratio
kpis.formula: (DME Spend + Cloud Migration Spend + New Development Spend) / Total IT Spend * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: Target: >40% for agencies in transformation; federal median: ~25%

kpis.name: Mean Time to Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
kpis.formula: AVG(Patch Deployment Date - CVE Publication Date) for CVSS >= 7.0
kpis.benchmarkRange: CISA BOD 22-01 target: 15 days for known exploited; best practice: <7 days

kpis.name: Critical Vulnerability Count
kpis.formula: COUNT(Assets with CVE CVSS >= 7.0 AND unpatched)
kpis.benchmarkRange: Best practice: trending to zero; CISA targets per BOD

kpis.name: Security Incident Rate
kpis.formula: COUNT(Security Incidents Reported) / Total Endpoint Count * 1,000
kpis.benchmarkRange: Federal: varies by agency; state/local: 10-30 per 1,000

kpis.name: Case Backlog Count
kpis.formula: SUM(Cases Pending > Standard Processing Time Threshold)
kpis.benchmarkRange: Best practice: <5% of annual volume; VA target: zero backlog

kpis.name: Average Case Processing Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Resolution Date - Receipt Date) for cases resolved in period
kpis.benchmarkRange: Statutory limits vary; best practice: <50% of statutory limit

kpis.name: Improper Payment Rate
kpis.formula: (Improper Payments $ / Total Program Outlays $) * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: OMB target: <2% for high-priority programs; Medicaid: 8-15%

kpis.name: Grant Utilization Rate
kpis.formula: (Actual Expenditures + Encumbrances) / Total Award Amount * 100%

kpis.name: Single Audit Finding Rate
kpis.formula: COUNT(Major Findings + Material Weaknesses) / Total Subjects Tested * 100%

kpis.name: Time-to-Hire (Critical Positions)
kpis.formula: AVG(Hire Date - Requisition Approval Date) for positions filled
kpis.benchmarkRange: Private sector benchmark: 30-45 days; public sector target: <90 days

kpis.name: Vacancy Rate (Priority Occupations)
kpis.formula: COUNT(Vacant Positions) / COUNT(Authorized Positions) * 100%

kpis.name: Procurement Cycle Time (RFP to Award)
kpis.formula: AVG(Contract Award Date - RFP Release Date)
kpis.benchmarkRange: Federal simplified: <60 days; complex IT: 180-365 days

kpis.name: Bid Protest Rate
kpis.formula: COUNT(Protests Filed) / COUNT(Award Decisions) * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: GAO sustain rate: ~15%; overall protest rate target: <3%

kpis.name: Citizen / Customer Satisfaction Score
kpis.formula: AVG(Survey Responses on 0-100 scale) for service transactions
kpis.benchmarkRange: Private sector leader: 75-85; public sector target: >65

kpis.name: Digital Service Adoption Rate
kpis.formula: Digital Transactions / Total Transactions * 100%

kpis.name: First-Contact Resolution Rate
kpis.formula: Cases Resolved on First Contact / Total Contacts * 100%

kpis.name: Asset Condition Index
kpis.formula: AVG(Asset Condition Rating) weighted by replacement value; scale 1-5 where 5=excellent

kpis.name: Deferred Maintenance Ratio
kpis.formula: Deferred Maintenance Backlog $ / Current Replacement Value $ * 100%

kpis.name: Audit Finding Rate
kpis.formula: COUNT(Findings) / COUNT(Audit Areas Tested) * 100%

kpis.name: Compliance Cost Ratio
kpis.formula: Compliance Staffing + Systems + Audit + Legal Costs / Total Administrative Budget * 100%

kpis.name: Budget Variance
kpis.formula: ABS(Actual Spend - Budgeted Spend) / Budgeted Spend * 100%

kpis.name: Emergency Response Time
kpis.formula: AVG(First Unit on Scene - Dispatch Time)
kpis.benchmarkRange: NFPA standard: 5:20 for fire; EMS: 8:59 for 90% of calls

kpis.name: FOIA / Public Records Processing Time
kpis.formula: AVG(Response Date - Request Date) for completed requests
kpis.benchmarkRange: Statutory: 20 business days (federal); best practice: <30 days

kpis.name: Cost Per Transaction
kpis.formula: Total Program Administrative Cost / Total Transactions Processed

kpis.name: Fraud Detection Rate
kpis.formula: Fraud Cases Detected and Confirmed / Total Cases Reviewed * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: Program dependent; target: increasing trend with analytics investment

kpis.name: Revenue Collection Rate
kpis.formula: Actual Collections / Billed / Assessed Revenue * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: Property tax: >95%; sales tax: >90%; federal tax gap: ~15%

kpis.name: Zero Trust Maturity Score
kpis.formula: AVG(Implementation Score across CISA Zero Trust Pillars: Identity, Device, Network, Application, Data) on 0-5 scale

kpis.name: Cloud Cost Per Workload
kpis.formula: Total Cloud Spend (IaaS + PaaS + SaaS) / Count of Production Workloads
kpis.benchmarkRange: Varies by workload type; target: declining trend with optimization

kpis.name: System Availability
kpis.formula: (Total Scheduled Uptime - Unplanned Downtime) / Total Scheduled Uptime * 100%

kpis.name: Grant Lapse Rate
kpis.formula: Unspent Grant Funds Returned to Funder / Total Grant Awards * 100%

kpis.name: Data Integration Coverage
kpis.formula: Systems with Active Data Exchange / Total Core Systems * 100%

kpis.name: Program Churn Rate
kpis.formula: (Disenrollments + Re-enrollments in period) / Average Enrollment * 100%

kpis.name: Infrastructure Replacement Rate
kpis.formula: Miles / Units / Miles Replaced or Rehabilitated / Total Inventory * 100%
kpis.benchmarkRange: Sustainable: >2% for linear assets; backlog elimination: >3%

kpis.name: Unaccounted-for Water
kpis.formula: (Water Produced - Water Billed + Authorized Unbilled) / Water Produced * 100%

kpis.name: Farebox Recovery Ratio
kpis.formula: Fare Revenue / Total Operating Cost * 100%

kpis.name: Pre-Trial Length of Stay
kpis.formula: AVG(Release or Disposition Date - Booking Date) for pre-trial detainees
kpis.benchmarkRange: Best practice: <14 days for non-violent; median: 20-30 days

kpis.name: Days to Obligate Grant Funds
kpis.formula: AVG(Obligation Date - Award Notification Date)
kpis.benchmarkRange: Target: <30 days for formula grants; discretionary: <90 days

kpis.name: Broadband Coverage Rate
kpis.formula: Locations with 100/20 Mbps or better / Total Locations * 100%

kpis.name: Ex Parte Renewal Rate
kpis.formula: Ex Parte Renewals Completed / Total Renewals Due * 100%

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Legacy O&M ratio >70%; system age >20 years; vendor EOL announced
Unpatched critical CVEs >30 days; no Zero Trust plan; phishing test failures >20%
Backlog growing >10% QoQ; processing time >statutory limit; overtime spending up
Improper payment rate >5% and trending up; OIG referrals increasing
Grant drawdown delays >60 days; single audit findings; historical lapse >5%
Vacancy rate >15% in critical roles; time-to-hire >120 days; contractor ratio >40%
Procurement lead time >200 days; protest rate >5%; sole-source increasing
Citizens repeating intake; duplicate records >10%; manual data exchanges via spreadsheet/fax
Citizen satisfaction <60%; call wait times >10 min; digital adoption <30%
Asset condition declining; deferred maintenance ratio >10%; safety incidents linked to condition
Repeat findings >30%; compliance staffing >10% of admin; control test failures
Budget revisions >2 per year; revenue forecast error >10%; fund balance drawdown
Unfulfilled mutual aid; PSAP outages; after-action recommendations unimplemented
FOIA backlog >1 year; no open data portal; records retention noncompliance
Enrollment declining >2% annually; state aid cuts; structural deficit growing
Eligibility time >45 days; churn >15%; CMS audit findings; state share pressure
Ridership <80% pre-pandemic; farebox recovery <20%; service cuts proposed
Main breaks increasing; lead line backlog; PFAS detection; rates rising >5%
Case disposition >180 days; pre-trial >50% of jail; overcrowding >90%
Cloud spend >budget 30%+; migration timeline >200% of plan; repatriation discussions

formulas.name: Legacy O&M Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if system inventory and budget actuals available; MEDIUM if using estimates; LOW if no system age data
formulas.exampleCalculation: Current 75% O&M, target 50%, $50M IT budget: (0.75 - 0.50) * $50M * 3 = $37.5M over 3 years

formulas.name: Cybersecurity Incident Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if incident history and cost data available; MEDIUM if using IBM/industry averages ($4.88M public sector 2024); LOW if no endpoint count
formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 20 incidents/1K, target 5, $2M avg cost, 10K endpoints: (20-5)/1000 * $2M * 10,000 = $300M over period

formulas.name: Case Backlog Elimination Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if case cost and staffing data available; MEDIUM if cost estimated from program budget/volume; LOW if no volume data
formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 50K backlog, target 5K, $200/case, $500K overtime avoided, 10 FTE @ $80K: 45K * $200 + $500K + $800K = $10.3M annual

formulas.name: Improper Payment Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if PERM/A-123 data available; MEDIUM if using PaymentAccuracy.gov; LOW if program outlay unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: $5B program, baseline 12% IP, target 6%, 30% recovery: $5B * 0.06 * 0.30 = $90M annual

formulas.name: Grant Lapse Avoidance Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if historical lapse data available; MEDIUM if using federal average; LOW if no award volume data
formulas.exampleCalculation: $100M awards, baseline 8% lapse, target 2%: $100M * 0.06 = $6M annual retained funding

formulas.name: Procurement Acceleration Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if procurement system data available; MEDIUM if using estimates; LOW if volume unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: Baseline 250 days, target 90 days, $200M volume, 4% cost of capital: (160/365) * $200M * 0.04 = $3.5M annual

formulas.name: Digital Channel Shift Cost Savings
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if channel cost data available; MEDIUM if using industry estimates ($50 paper vs $5 digital); LOW if transaction volume unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: 1M transactions, 20%->60% digital, $75 paper vs $5 digital: 400K * $70 = $28M annual

formulas.name: Workforce Efficiency Value
requiredInputs: Contractor hours converted to FTE (count * loaded cost delta)
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if HRIS and payroll data available; MEDIUM if using estimates; LOW if no staffing analysis
formulas.exampleCalculation: $1M overtime reduction, 20 contractors @ $150K->15 FTE @ $100K = $1M savings, 5% productivity on 50 filled roles @ $80K = $200K

formulas.name: Preventive vs Reactive Maintenance Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if CMMS data available; MEDIUM if using FEMA/ASCE ratios; LOW if asset inventory incomplete
formulas.exampleCalculation: $10K reactive vs $3K preventive, 1,000 assets, $2M avoided emergency: ($7K * 1,000) + $2M = $9M

formulas.name: Inter-Agency Data Integration Value
requiredInputs: Hours of duplicate entry eliminated annually
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if time-motion study available; MEDIUM if estimated from system logs; LOW if purely speculative
formulas.exampleCalculation: 50K hours eliminated @ $50/hr = $2.5M + $1M faster decisions + $500K errors = $4M annual

formulas.name: FOIA / Records Processing Efficiency
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if cost tracking available; MEDIUM if using DOJ estimates; LOW if volume unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: $2,000/request->$500, 5K requests, $500K litigation avoidance: ($1,500 * 5K) + $500K = $8M

formulas.name: Medicaid Churn Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if state administrative data available; MEDIUM if using KFF estimates; LOW if enrollment unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: 20%->10% churn on 1M enrollees, $100/enrollee admin cost, $200 gap cost: 100K * $300 = $30M

formulas.name: Transit Operating Cost Optimization
requiredInputs: Baseline cost per vehicle revenue mile
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if NTD data available; MEDIUM if using peer benchmarks; LOW if no operating data
formulas.exampleCalculation: $12/VRM->$10, 10M VRM, $2M revenue recovery: ($2 * 10M) + $2M = $22M

formulas.name: Water Loss Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if water audit available; MEDIUM if using AWWA estimates; LOW if production data unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: 25%->15% UFW, 1B gallons, $4/1K gal, $1M deferred: (10% * 1B * $4/1K) + $1M = $5M

formulas.name: Pre-Trial Length of Stay Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if jail cost accounting available; MEDIUM if using Vera Institute estimates ($100-$200/day); LOW if population unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: 30->14 days, $150/day, 200 avg population: 16 * $150 * 200 * 365 = $175.2M (with confidence adjustment)

formulas.name: Revenue Collection Rate Improvement
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if revenue records available; MEDIUM if using tax gap estimates; LOW if no assessment data
formulas.exampleCalculation: $500M assessed, 90%->95% collection: $500M * 0.05 = $25M

formulas.name: Single Audit Finding Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if audit history and cost data available; MEDIUM if estimated; LOW if no grant history
formulas.exampleCalculation: 10 findings @ $50K remediation + $200K suspension risk = $700K

formulas.name: Broadband Deployment Cost Efficiency
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM (BEAD program new, limited historical data); LOW if no location data
formulas.exampleCalculation: $5K->$3.5K per location, 50K locations, $1M compliance risk: ($1.5K * 50K) + $1M = $76M

formulas.name: School / Campus Energy & Facility Cost Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if energy audit available; MEDIUM if using EPA/Energy Star estimates; LOW if no utility data
formulas.exampleCalculation: $10M energy, 20% efficiency, $2M deferred: $2M + $2M = $4M

formulas.name: Pension / OPEB Contribution Optimization
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM (requires actuarial analysis); LOW if no actuarial valuation
formulas.exampleCalculation: $50M current->$45M optimized + $2M return + $500K admin = $7.5M

formulas.name: Cloud Spend Optimization
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if cloud billing data available; MEDIUM if using Flexera estimates (30% waste); LOW if no spend data
formulas.exampleCalculation: $10M cloud, 25% optimization, $2M avoided overrun: $2.5M + $2M = $4.5M

formulas.name: Ex Parte Renewal Efficiency Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if eligibility system data available; MEDIUM if using state averages; LOW if no renewal volume
formulas.exampleCalculation: 200K renewals shifted, $25 manual cost, $1M churn value, $500K compliance: $5M + $1M + $500K = $6.5M

formulas.name: 311 / Citizen Request Processing Value
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM if 311 system data available; LOW if no cost tracking
formulas.exampleCalculation: $85->$25 per request, 500K requests, 5 FTE @ $80K: ($60 * 500K) + $400K = $30.4M

formulas.name: Smart City IoT Operational Value
formulas.formulaExpression: V = Energy_Savings + Traffic_Efficiency_Value + Safety_Improvement_Value + Predictive_Maintenance_Value - Sensor_Network_TCO
formulas.confidenceRules: LOW (emerging, limited long-term data; city-specific); MEDIUM if pilot data available
formulas.exampleCalculation: $1M energy + $2M traffic + $500K safety + $1.5M maintenance - $2M TCO = $3M net

formulas.name: Acquisition Workforce Productivity Improvement
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM if FPDS data and workforce data available; LOW if headcount unknown
formulas.exampleCalculation: 8->12 procurements/1102, 500 1102s, $2M avg value, 10% compression: 2,000 * $2M * 0.10 = $400M (simplified)

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: O&M budget >70% of total IT; system age >20 years; vendor EOL announced
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Agency trapped in legacy maintenance with limited modernization capacity; skills and vendor risk concentrated; modernization funding squeezed

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Unpatched critical CVE count >10; mean patch time >30 days; CISA KEV list overlap
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Security operations understaffed or under-resourced; change management bottlenecks; potential for breach or compliance finding

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Pending cases >90 days growing 10%+ QoQ; processing time >statutory threshold; overtime budget overrun
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Demand exceeds processing capacity; workflow bottlenecks; potential statutory or court-order violation

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Improper payment rate >5% and increasing YoY; OIG referral volume up; media/GAO attention
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Program integrity controls inadequate; eligibility verification gaps; potential for payment clawbacks, sanctions, or legislative attention

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Days to drawdown >60 days; single audit findings recurring; subrecipient monitoring gaps documented
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Grant management systems and processes inadequate; risk of fund lapse; audit escalation; potential suspension

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Vacancy rate >15% in priority occupations; time-to-hire >120 days; contractor ratio >40% of IT spend
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Compensation non-competitive; hiring process broken; knowledge concentration risk; service delivery risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Procurement lead time >200 days; protest rate >5%; sole-source justifications increasing
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Requirements development weak; market research inadequate; acquisition workforce overwhelmed; mission capability delayed

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Citizens required to provide same data to multiple agencies; duplicate records >10% in master system; fax/spreadsheet data exchange observed
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: No enterprise data architecture; no master person/index; integration projects failed or absent; administrative waste high

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Complaint volume increasing 15%+ YoY; call wait times >10 minutes; first-call resolution <60%; digital transactions <30%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Service delivery failing citizen expectations; channel strategy inadequate; potential for political/legislative pressure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Asset condition rating declining; deferred maintenance backlog growing >inflation; safety incidents linked to asset condition
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Lifecycle maintenance underfunded; reactive maintenance dominating; capital planning inadequate; failure risk rising

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Repeat findings >30% of total findings; same control deficiencies >2 years; management letter citations
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Internal controls weak; remediation under-resourced; potential for escalation to material weakness or adverse opinion

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Budget revisions >2 per fiscal year; hiring freeze invoked; reliance on one-time funds >20% of budget
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Revenue forecasting unreliable; structural deficit present; spending flexibility constrained; service cuts likely

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Mutual aid requests unfulfilled; after-action recommendations >50% unimplemented; PSAP reliability <99.5%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Interoperability and coordination systems inadequate; preparedness underfunded; single points of failure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Backlog >1 year; processing time >90 days; requestor litigation filed
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Records management systems inadequate; manual search processes; retention policy gaps; legal exposure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Enrollment declining >2% annually for 3+ years; state aid per pupil declining; program closures announced
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Demographic shift or competition eroding base; fixed costs creating structural deficit; strategic repositioning needed

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: State share growing faster than state revenue; CMS audit findings; eligibility determination time >45 days
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Program crowding out other priorities; administrative systems inadequate; compliance risk with CMS

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Ridership <80% of pre-pandemic for 2+ years; farebox recovery <20%; service cuts proposed
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Revenue model broken; operating subsidy dependency increasing; service-quality spiral risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Health-based violations in last 2 years; main breaks >10 per 100 miles; lead service lines present
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Infrastructure at end of life; regulatory enforcement risk; public health liability; rate pressure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Pre-trial >50% of jail population; jail occupancy >90%; case disposition time >180 days
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Case processing system broken; pretrial services inadequate; potential consent decree; capacity expansion needed

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: 1102 vacancy rate >15%; IT acquisition protest rate >5%; cost growth >20% on major programs
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Workforce cannot meet acquisition demand; skills mismatch with modern IT; program delivery at risk

signalRules.signalName: High Program Churn / Ex Parte Low
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Churn rate >15%; ex parte renewal rate <30%; CMS unwinding metrics show high disenrollment
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Eligibility systems and processes causing coverage gaps; administrative burden high; health/access outcomes compromised

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Safety drills noncompliant; incident response untested >12 months; mental health referral backlog
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Safety infrastructure and procedures inadequate; coordination with first responders weak; liability exposure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Cloud spend >budget 30%+; migration timeline >200% of plan; FedRAMP ATO >12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Migration planning inadequate; skills gaps; security authorization bottleneck; potential repatriation

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Unserved locations >10% of jurisdiction; BEAD milestone at risk; low ACP adoption
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Digital divide perpetuating inequity; federal compliance deadline pressure; deployment coordination complex

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Funded ratio <70%; ARC payment <100%; credit rating downgrade or negative outlook
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Long-term fiscal health deteriorating; current services being crowded out; actuarial assumptions may be optimistic

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Agency operating under CR >3 months; shutdown plan activated in last 2 years; new starts frozen
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Multi-year planning impossible; contractor stop-work orders likely; employee morale and retention risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: NARA appraisal backlog; records retention schedules outdated >5 years; FOIA redaction manual only
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Information governance immature; legal discovery risk; NARA compliance failure; e-discovery cost exposure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: No FHIR adoption; NIEM not implemented; HIE disconnected from agency systems
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Federal data sharing mandates unfulfilled; care coordination impaired; duplicate services rendered

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: AI use documented without governance framework; no bias testing; procurement lacks AI clauses
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Regulatory and reputational risk from algorithmic decision-making; EO 14110 compliance gap; equity concerns

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Zero Trust maturity score <2.0; no phishing-resistant MFA; legacy VPN still primary access
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: OMB M-22-09 and CISA requirements unfulfilled; identity-based attacks likely; compliance gap for federal agencies

evidenceSources.name: U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO) Reports
evidenceSources.description: Independent, non-partisan audits and evaluations of federal programs. Provides high-confidence evidence on waste, fraud, duplication, and program effectiveness.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: GAO.gov; searchable by agency, topic, report number

evidenceSources.name: Office of Inspector General (OIG) Reports
evidenceSources.description: Agency-specific audits, inspections, and investigations. Reveals internal control weaknesses, fraud cases, and program integrity issues.
evidenceSources.updateFrequency: Quarterly to annual reports; ad-hoc for investigations
applicableSegments: All federal agencies; some state OIG equivalents

evidenceSources.name: Federal Budget Justifications / Congressional Budget Justifications (CBJs)
evidenceSources.description: Agency annual budget requests with program-level detail, performance metrics, and proposed changes.

evidenceSources.name: Federal Spending & Contract Data
evidenceSources.description: Transaction-level federal award and contract data. FPDS for contracts; SAM for grants and assistance.

evidenceSources.name: National Association of State CIOs (NASCIO) Annual Survey
evidenceSources.description: Annual state CIO survey covering priorities, spending, workforce, and digital government.

evidenceSources.name: OMB Improper Payments Data
evidenceSources.description: Annual improper payment rates by program, root causes, and corrective actions. High-priority programs listed.
applicableSegments: Federal benefit programs; state-administered federal programs

evidenceSources.name: Single Audit Repository
evidenceSources.description: Repository of A-133/Single Audit reports for entities receiving >$750K in federal awards.

evidenceSources.name: U.S. Census Bureau Data
evidenceSources.description: Population, economic, demographic, housing, and business data.
evidenceSources.updateFrequency: Decennial census; ACS annual; economic census periodic

evidenceSources.name: ASCE Infrastructure Report Card
evidenceSources.description: Quadrennial assessment of U.S. infrastructure condition, funding needs, and investment gap.

evidenceSources.name: Transit Industry Data (APTA + FTA)
evidenceSources.description: Ridership, operating statistics, and financial data for U.S. transit systems.

evidenceSources.name: KFF Medicaid / Health Policy Research
evidenceSources.description: Non-partisan health policy research with state-level Medicaid data, enrollment trends, and financing.

evidenceSources.name: National Center for Education Statistics
evidenceSources.description: Primary federal entity for education data. Includes enrollment, finance, graduation rates, and staffing.

evidenceSources.name: EPA Drinking Water Data
evidenceSources.description: Safe Drinking Water Information System (violations) and Drinking Water Infrastructure Needs Survey.
evidenceSources.updateFrequency: Needs survey every 4 years; SDWIS near real-time

evidenceSources.name: Government Procurement Documents
evidenceSources.description: RFPs, RFIs, sole-source justifications, contract awards. Reveal requirements, incumbent vendors, and spending patterns.
evidenceSources.accessMethod: SAM.gov; agency procurement sites; state e-procurement portals

evidenceSources.name: Comprehensive Annual Financial Reports
evidenceSources.description: Audited financial statements for state and local governments. Include fund balance, pension liabilities, and debt.

buyingTriggers.name: Annual Appropriation / Budget Authorization
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: New fiscal year budget approved; new program funding authorized; supplemental appropriation
buyingTriggers.typicalTiming: Federal: Oct 1; State: July 1 or as defined; Local: varies
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: New RFPs released; Q1 and Q4 peak activity; multi-year funding enables large projects

buyingTriggers.name: Critical Audit Finding with Management Direction
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: GAO High Risk List update; OIG report with management commitment to remediate
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Directed procurement; sole-source justification possible for urgent remediation; IG may monitor

buyingTriggers.name: Security Incident or Data Breach
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Confirmed breach; ransomware attack; CISA alert requiring action; classified spill
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Emergency procurement; sole-source justified; accelerated timeline; may bypass normal competition

buyingTriggers.name: New Federal Grant Award or NOFO Release
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Agency receives new grant award; NOFO published with specific technology/compliance requirements
buyingTriggers.typicalTiming: 30-90 days post-award; NOFO response windows 45-90 days
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Grant-funded procurement; compliance requirements dictate vendor selection; pass-through rules apply

buyingTriggers.name: Compliance Deadline or New Regulation
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: New regulation effective date; consent decree deadline; NARA 2025 electronic records mandate
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Compliance-driven procurement; requirements tightly specified; limited vendor pool if specialized

buyingTriggers.name: New Agency Leadership (Political or Career)
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: New Secretary/Administrator/Director appointed; new CIO/CFO/CISO hired
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Strategic reviews; potential cancellation of pending procurements; new priorities create new opportunities

buyingTriggers.name: Incumbent Contract End / Option Exhaustion
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Contract within 12-18 months of expiration; option years declining to exercise; performance issues documented
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Full and open competition likely; incumbent advantage vs. fresh look; past performance critical

buyingTriggers.name: Federal IT Modernization Initiative
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: TMF funding announcement; MGT Act approval; FITARA score improvement mandate; agency CIO modernization plan
buyingTriggers.typicalTiming: TMF proposals quarterly; MGT boards meet periodically
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Working capital fund mechanisms; incremental funding; technical evaluation focused on modernization approach

buyingTriggers.name: Federal or State Disaster Declaration
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Major disaster declaration (FEMA); public health emergency (HHS); cyber incident emergency
buyingTriggers.typicalTiming: Immediate; Stafford Act processes; recovery funding follows 30-90 days
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Emergency procurement; FEMA PA/S grants; streamlined competition; sole-source permissible

buyingTriggers.name: New Law or Reauthorization
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: IIJA/IRA/CHIPS passage; Farm Bill reauthorization; Medicaid expansion; education funding reauthorization
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Large-scale program implementation; multiple procurements; fast-tracked requirements development

buyingTriggers.name: Judicial Order or Settlement
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Court order mandating reform; DOJ settlement (ADA, civil rights); class-action consent decree
buyingTriggers.typicalTiming: Immediate upon order; compliance timeline set by court
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Court-appointed monitor may review; requirements tightly specified; compliance timeline non-negotiable

buyingTriggers.name: System / Platform End-of-Life Announcement
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Vendor announces EOL/EOS for critical system; hardware no longer supported; OS version deprecated
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Replacement procurement; migration services; may be sole-source if only vendor

buyingTriggers.name: Retirement Eligibility Surge
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: >20% of critical workforce eligible for retirement within 3 years; knowledge management crisis
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Staff augmentation; knowledge capture systems; training technology; succession planning services

buyingTriggers.name: Post-Shutdown / Full-Year Appropriation
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CR ends with full appropriations; shutdown ends with retroactive funding
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Compressed procurement timelines; use-it-or-lose-it spending; simplified acquisition threshold increases

buyingTriggers.name: Breach Notification Law Trigger
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: State or federal breach notification law expansion; HIPAA breach threshold reduction
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Security technology procurement; incident response services; legal/compliance consulting

buyingTriggers.name: Voter-Approved Infrastructure Funding
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: General obligation bond passage; sales tax increment approval; special district formation
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Design-bid-build or P3 procurement; engineering services; construction management; technology systems

buyingTriggers.name: External Accreditation or Audit Visit
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Higher ed accreditation visit scheduled; CMS audit announced; Joint Commission survey
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Remediation services; documentation systems; compliance technology; consulting

buyingTriggers.name: Cloud-First or Digital-First Policy Mandate
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: New administration cloud policy; Smart City resolution; digital transformation executive order
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Cloud migration services; SaaS procurement; data center consolidation; hybrid cloud architecture

buyingTriggers.name: Federal Data Interoperability Requirement
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: CMS FHIR mandate; NIEM adoption requirement; CJIS integration; HIE connectivity deadline
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Integration platform procurement; standards consulting; API development; legacy system interface

buyingTriggers.name: Program Eligibility Expansion
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Medicaid expansion; SNAP eligibility broadened; housing voucher expansion; refugee resettlement
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Eligibility system scaling; call center expansion; case management capacity; training

buyingTriggers.name: Federal Formula Fund Allocation (IIJA, IRA, BIL)
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: FHWA/FTA/EPA formula fund apportionment notice; BEAD allocation
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Design/engineering procurement; construction; technology systems; must obligate within timeframe

buyingTriggers.name: Agency Consolidation or Shared Services Mandate
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Administration consolidation order; shared services mandate (e.g., Payroll, HR, IT); merger of agencies
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Enterprise system procurement; integration services; change management; data migration

buyingTriggers.name: P3 or Alternative Delivery Method Enabled
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: State P3 enabling legislation; federal APD approval; availability payment authorization
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Complex, multi-year procurement; RFQ/PDB process; long evaluation; legal/financial advisory

buyingTriggers.name: CISA Binding Operational Directive or OMB Memo
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: BOD issuance (e.g., BOD 22-01, BOD 23-02); OMB M-22-09 enforcement milestone
affectedSegments: Federal; cascading to state/local via grants
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Identity platform; endpoint detection; network segmentation; privileged access management

buyingTriggers.name: Outcome / Performance-Based Budget Requirement
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: State legislature mandates performance budgeting; GAO recommends performance frameworks; Results Act enforcement
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Performance management systems; data analytics; dashboard/reporting tools; consulting

personas.name: Chief Information Officer
personas.role: Agency IT leader; owns technology strategy, budget, and operations
dislikedClaims: Rip and replace without migration plan

personas.name: Chief Financial Officer / Comptroller
personas.role: Agency financial leader; owns budget, accounting, audit, and fiscal compliance

personas.name: Program Director / Agency Head
personas.role: Mission delivery leader; owns program outcomes, policy implementation, and stakeholder relations

personas.name: Chief Information Security Officer
personas.role: Security leader; owns cybersecurity strategy, operations, compliance, and incident response
dislikedClaims: Secure by default marketing without FedRAMP

personas.name: Inspector General / Internal Auditor
personas.role: Independent oversight; audits, investigates, and inspects agency programs

personas.name: Budget Officer / Grants Management Specialist
personas.role: Budget formulation, execution, and grant administration

personas.name: Contracting Officer / Chief Procurement Officer
personas.role: Procures goods and services; ensures competition, compliance, and best value

personas.name: Frontline Case Worker / Eligibility Specialist
personas.role: Direct service delivery; determines eligibility; manages caseloads
dislikedClaims: Efficiency that eliminates jobs without notice
Systems that don't reflect field reality

personas.name: Public Works Director / Utility General Manager
personas.role: Owns infrastructure assets, operations, maintenance, and capital planning

personas.name: Education Institution Leader
personas.role: Academic and operational leader; owns enrollment, curriculum, finance, and accreditation
dislikedClaims: Retention technology that ignores human factors

personas.name: Emergency Management Director
personas.role: Coordinates preparedness, response, recovery, and mitigation
dislikedClaims: Technology that doesn't work in disasters

personas.name: Chief Data Officer / Analytics Director
personas.role: Data strategy, governance, quality, and analytics enablement
personas.seniority: Director to C-level (emerging role in public sector)
dislikedClaims: AI will solve it without data foundation

personas.name: Chief Acquisition Officer / Senior Procurement Executive
personas.role: Oversees acquisition strategy, workforce, and policy

personas.name: Elected Official / Governing Board Member
personas.role: Sets policy, approves budget, provides oversight, represents constituents
dislikedClaims: Solutions that require tax increases without clear ROI

discoveryQuestions.question: How many times did you revise your budget last fiscal year, and what drove the largest revision?
If you had to free up 15% of your administrative budget to redirect to direct mission delivery, where would you find it?
What percentage of your IT budget goes to maintaining systems that are more than 15 years old?
What was your last material audit finding, and is it still open?
How many of your critical technical staff are eligible for retirement in the next three years?
How many cases/claims/transactions are pending beyond your service standard right now?
Do you know your all-in cost per transaction by channel (paper, phone, digital, in-person)?
How many times does a citizen have to provide the same information to access multiple services?
What percentage of your federal awards typically lapse or go unspent?
From the time you identify a need, how long until contract award? What's the longest step?
When did you last measure citizen satisfaction, and what was the trend?
What percentage of your transactions are completed without staff intervention?
What generates the most complaints to your office — and what's your root cause analysis?
How many critical vulnerabilities are unpatched in your environment right now, and why?
Where are you on the CISA Zero Trust maturity model, and what's your biggest gap?
What percentage of your workloads are cloud-hosted, and how much are you spending on cloud vs. plan?
Can your core systems exchange data automatically with other agencies, or does someone email spreadsheets?
What percentage of last year's audit findings were repeats from prior years?
How much of your administrative budget is consumed by compliance activities?
How long does it take to respond to a routine FOIA/public records request, and what's the backlog?

objections.name: Budget Constraints Objection
objections.pattern: There's no money in this fiscal year; we're under a hiring freeze; CR prevents new starts
objections.rootCause: Actual appropriation limits; uncertainty; competing priorities; risk aversion
objections.responseStrategy: Identify alternative funding (grants, TMF, MGT, fee revenue); phase implementation; demonstrate cost avoidance > cost; find sponsor with budget
objections.validationNeeded: Actual vs. perceived constraint; existence of contingency reserves; reprogramming authority

objections.name: Procurement Friction Objection
objections.pattern: By the time we could award, the need changes; FAR compliance is burdensome; protests kill timelines
objections.rootCause: Acquisition workforce shortage; risk aversion; protest trauma; requirements ambiguity
objections.responseStrategy: Pre-position via BPA/IDIQ; simplify SOW with performance-based specs; offer acquisition support; propose pilot/SaaS
objections.validationNeeded: Actual protest history; acquisition workforce capacity; existing contract vehicles

objections.name: Incumbent Lock-in Objection
objections.pattern: Our current vendor knows the system; switching costs are too high; we have a sole-source justification
objections.rootCause: Technical debt; knowledge concentration; transition cost uncertainty; complacency
objections.responseStrategy: Quantify total cost of incumbent (including change orders, schedule slippage); offer transition services; phased migration; data portability guarantees
objections.validationNeeded: Contract end date; option years; past performance issues; actual switching cost analysis

objections.name: External Dependency Objection
objections.pattern: We're waiting for the new administration's priorities; legislation pending; budget not finalized
objections.rootCause: Political uncertainty; multi-year planning impossible; risk of starting without authorization
objections.responseStrategy: Position for next cycle; help shape requirements in interim; demonstrate pilot value; build coalition
objections.validationNeeded: Actual timeline dependency; pre-positioning opportunity; who controls the decision

objections.name: Stakeholder Resistance Objection
objections.pattern: The union opposes automation; staff fear job loss; elected officials won't support
objections.rootCause: Job security fears; change fatigue; political sensitivity; past failed implementations
objections.responseStrategy: Co-design with stakeholders; commit to redeployment not reduction; phased rollout with training; show peer success; union partnership
objections.validationNeeded: Actual union position; historical change success/failure; key stakeholder map

objections.name: Past Failure Objection
objections.pattern: We did an ERP/modernization/cloud migration and it was a disaster; we're not doing that again
objections.rootCause: Actual failed project; scar tissue; risk aversion; leadership memory; vendor blame
objections.responseStrategy: Acknowledge failure; diagnose root cause (often requirements, not technology); propose different approach; start with pilot; reference peer recovery
objections.validationNeeded: Actual failure analysis; what was different; who was involved; lessons learned documented

objections.name: Mandated Procurement Vehicle Objection
objections.pattern: We must use GSA Schedule / SEWP / state contract / cooperative purchasing
objections.rootCause: Actual policy requirement; preference for streamlined procurement; category management mandate
objections.responseStrategy: Get on required vehicle; partner with incumbent on vehicle; demonstrate vehicle limitations; seek exception
objections.validationNeeded: Actual mandate vs. preference; vehicle availability; exception process

objections.name: Government-Specific Proof Requirement
objections.pattern: Show me another [state/city/agency] like us that succeeded; private sector examples don't count
objections.rootCause: Unique compliance environment; risk aversion; credibility gap for private-sector vendors
objections.responseStrategy: Provide public sector references; arrange peer visit; offer pilot with measurable outcomes; leverage cooperative networks
objections.validationNeeded: What constitutes 'like us'; who they respect as peer; appetite for pilot

objections.name: Security/Compliance Gate Objection
objections.pattern: CISO won't approve cloud/SaaS/AI; FISMA/FedRAMP compliance too complex; our security requirements are unique
objections.rootCause: Actual security requirements; risk aversion; compliance burden; knowledge gaps about modern approaches
objections.responseStrategy: Lead with compliance (FedRAMP, StateRAMP, SOC 2); offer security architecture review; phased authorization; CISO-to-CISO reference
objections.validationNeeded: Actual security requirements document; FedRAMP/authorization status; who holds approval authority

objections.name: In-House Development Preference
objections.pattern: Our IT team can build that; we've always done it in-house; custom is better than commercial
objections.rootCause: Insourcing preference; past custom success; vendor distrust; perceived cost savings; workforce preservation
objections.responseStrategy: Compare total cost of ownership; highlight maintenance burden; show opportunity cost; propose hybrid (configure vs. build); demonstrate time-to-value gap
objections.validationNeeded: Actual in-house capacity; historical build success rate; maintenance backlog; opportunity cost analysis

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
technologySystems.description: Core financial, HR, procurement, and budgeting system. Often legacy (PeopleSoft, SAP, Oracle EBS) or modern cloud (Workday, Oracle Cloud, SAP S/4HANA).

technologySystems.name: Case Management Platform
technologySystems.description: Tracks cases, clients, services, and outcomes across human services, health, justice, and procurement.

technologySystems.name: Grants Management System
technologySystems.description: Manages grant lifecycle from application through closeout, including compliance, reporting, and subrecipient monitoring.

technologySystems.name: E-Procurement / Contract Management
technologySystems.description: Supports solicitation, evaluation, award, and contract management. Includes vendor registration, e-bidding, and spend analytics.

technologySystems.name: Security Operations (SIEM/SOAR/XDR)
technologySystems.description: Security information and event management, orchestration, and extended detection and response.

technologySystems.name: Identity and Access Management (IAM)
technologySystems.description: Authentication, authorization, and provisioning. Critical for Zero Trust and digital service delivery.

technologySystems.name: Government / Citizen CRM
technologySystems.description: Manages citizen interactions across channels (phone, web, mobile, social, in-person). Includes 311, case routing, and knowledge base.

technologySystems.name: Enterprise GIS Platform
technologySystems.description: Spatial data management, mapping, and analytics for land use, utilities, emergency response, and service planning.

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Asset / Work Management
technologySystems.description: Manages asset inventory, condition, maintenance scheduling, and work orders for infrastructure and facilities.

technologySystems.name: Learning Management System
technologySystems.description: Delivers, tracks, and manages training and professional development. Includes compliance training and certification.

technologySystems.name: Electronic Health Record / Health Information Exchange
technologySystems.description: Patient health records, clinical workflows, and health information exchange. Critical for Medicaid, VA, public health clinics.

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Content / Records Management
technologySystems.description: Document capture, storage, retrieval, retention, and FOIA/redaction processing.

technologySystems.name: Data Warehouse / BI / Analytics Platform
technologySystems.description: Centralized data repository with reporting, dashboards, and analytics capabilities.

technologySystems.name: Contact Center Platform
technologySystems.description: Phone, chat, email, and SMS interaction management with routing, IVR, and workforce optimization.

technologySystems.name: Fleet and Mobile Asset Management
technologySystems.description: GPS tracking, vehicle diagnostics, route optimization, and maintenance scheduling for government fleets.

technologySystems.name: SCADA / Industrial Control Systems
technologySystems.description: Real-time monitoring and control of water, wastewater, energy, and transit infrastructure.

technologySystems.name: Student Information System
technologySystems.description: Student enrollment, scheduling, attendance, grades, and state reporting for K-12 and higher ed.

technologySystems.name: Computer-Aided Dispatch / Records Management
technologySystems.description: 911 call taking, dispatch, incident records, evidence management, and mobile data for law enforcement and EMS.

technologySystems.name: Cloud Infrastructure & Platform Services
technologySystems.description: Compute, storage, networking, and platform services. FedRAMP/StateRAMP authorized for government use.

technologySystems.name: Human Capital / Workforce Management
technologySystems.description: HR, payroll, benefits, time/attendance, recruiting, and performance management.

regulatoryFactors.name: Federal Information Security Modernization Act
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 44 U.S.C. Chapter 35, Subchapter II; OMB M-20-31, M-21-31, M-22-09
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All federal agencies; state/local via grant conditions
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Continuous; annual FISMA reporting to Congress
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: GAO/OIG findings; Congressional scrutiny; potential funding restrictions; CISO accountability
affectedSegments: All Federal; State/Local cascading via HHS, DHS, DOT grants

regulatoryFactors.name: Federal Acquisition Regulation
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 48 CFR Chapter 1; agency supplements (DFARS, VAAR, etc.)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Protest sustain; GAO/OIG findings; contract termination; debarment; False Claims Act exposure

regulatoryFactors.name: Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements
regulatoryFactors.applicability: All entities receiving federal grants and cooperative agreements
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Continuous; single audit due 9 months after fiscal year end
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Audit findings; cost disallowance; suspension; termination; debarment

regulatoryFactors.name: Accessibility Requirements
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Section 508 of Rehabilitation Act (29 U.S.C. 794d); WCAG 2.1 AA
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Federal agencies; state/local under ADA; education under Section 504/ADA
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: DOJ enforcement; complaints; litigation; contract termination

regulatoryFactors.name: NIST Cybersecurity Standards
regulatoryFactors.regulation: NIST CSF; SP 800-53 Rev 5; SP 800-171; SP 800-207 (Zero Trust)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: FISMA deficiencies; audit findings; breach liability; procurement disqualification

regulatoryFactors.name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Health care providers, health plans, clearinghouses; state Medicaid agencies
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Civil monetary penalties ($137-$2.067M per violation tier); criminal penalties; corrective action plans

regulatoryFactors.name: Freedom of Information Act / State Equivalents
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Federal agencies; state/local agencies per state law
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Litigation; attorney fee awards; media exposure; administrative sanctions

regulatoryFactors.name: National Archives and Records Administration Requirements
regulatoryFactors.regulation: 44 U.S.C. Chapters 21, 29, 31; NARA regulations; M-23-07 (electronic records)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Federal agencies; state/local records laws vary
regulatoryFactors.deadline: M-23-07: June 30, 2024 for full electronic records
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: OIG findings; GAO high-risk; records loss; litigation exposure

regulatoryFactors.name: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program
regulatoryFactors.regulation: FedRAMP baselines (Low, Moderate, High); StateRAMP for state/local
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Federal cloud services; StateRAMP for participating states
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Continuous; ATO required before cloud service use
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Prohibition on cloud use; FISMA finding; procurement cancellation

regulatoryFactors.name: State-Specific Procurement Regulations
regulatoryFactors.regulation: Varies by state (e.g., California PUC, Texas GPC, New York OSC)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: State agencies, local governments, authorities, districts
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Protest; bid rejection; debarment; criminal liability for fraud

regulatoryFactors.name: Water Quality and Drinking Water Regulations
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Water utilities, wastewater facilities, industrial dischargers
regulatoryFactors.deadline: Continuous; MCL compliance; LT2/DBP rules; lead service line replacement mandates
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Enforcement actions; consent decrees; civil penalties; criminal liability for knowing violations

regulatoryFactors.name: Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Federal agencies; guidance for state/local; contractors
regulatoryFactors.deadline: 90-365 days for various agency actions per EO
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: OMB guidance; procurement restrictions; reputational; emerging regulatory

competitorFactors.name: Federal IT Contractor Concentration
competitorFactors.description: Federal market dominated by large integrators (Leidos, GDIT, Booz Allen, CACI, SAIC, Peraton, CGI Federal). Small business set-asides create tiered competition.
competitorFactors.marketDynamics: Consolidation (M&A activity); category management reducing vendor count; SB set-aside requirements; OTAs for innovation
competitorFactors.threatLevel: HIGH for new entrants; MEDIUM for established players
competitorFactors.mitigation: Differentiation via specialized expertise; partner strategy; OTA/CMO vehicles; state/local expansion

competitorFactors.name: State and Local Market Fragmentation
competitorFactors.description: 50 state markets with different procurement laws, 19,000 municipalities. Fragmented vendor landscape with regional players.
competitorFactors.marketDynamics: Tyler Technologies, CentralSquare, Granicus consolidating; cooperatives (HGAC, NCPA) enabling scale; SaaS lowering entry barriers
competitorFactors.mitigation: Cooperative purchasing; SaaS delivery; partner with regional firms; vertical specialization

competitorFactors.name: Incumbent Advantage and Switching Costs
competitorFactors.description: Long-tenured incumbents (e.g., legacy Medicaid MMIS, ERP systems) benefit from deep institutional knowledge and high switching costs.
competitorFactors.marketDynamics: Contract extensions common; rebids favor incumbents; data portability emerging as differentiator
competitorFactors.threatLevel: HIGH for replacement plays; LOW for new capability
competitorFactors.mitigation: Total cost of ownership analysis; transition services; modular replacement; data portability guarantees

competitorFactors.name: Open Source and Low-Cost Competition
competitorFactors.description: Pressure from open source (Moodle, Linux, PostgreSQL) and low-cost SaaS alternatives in budget-constrained environments.
competitorFactors.marketDynamics: Total cost of ownership often favors commercial support; security requirements limit open source; commercial open source hybrid emerging
competitorFactors.threatLevel: MEDIUM for commoditized functions; LOW for mission-critical systems
competitorFactors.mitigation: Demonstrate total cost; compliance certification; support SLAs; security validation

competitorFactors.name: Hyperscaler Direct Engagement
competitorFactors.description: AWS, Azure, Google engage directly with agencies, bypassing traditional integrators. Marketplace models reduce partner role.
competitorFactors.marketDynamics: Marketplace procurement growing; hyperscaler professional services expanding; MSP/SI role evolving to multi-cloud management
competitorFactors.threatLevel: MEDIUM-HIGH for infrastructure plays; LOW for application layer
competitorFactors.mitigation: Multi-cloud expertise; application modernization; security overlay; managed services; data/platform independence

competitorFactors.name: AI and Emerging Technology Vendors
competitorFactors.description: AI-native vendors (Palantir, Anduril, etc.) and emerging tech startups competing for innovation-oriented procurement.
competitorFactors.marketDynamics: OTAs and SBIR/STTR enable non-traditional entrants; AI hype creates noise; procurement caution on unproven tech
competitorFactors.threatLevel: MEDIUM for innovation-focused agencies; LOW for risk-averse
competitorFactors.mitigation: Demonstrate proven outcomes; secure FedRAMP/authorization; reference public sector wins; partner vs. compete

valueDomains.name: Mission Effectiveness
valueDomains.description: Improving program outcomes, service quality, and citizen/client results.
valueDomains.confidence: Varies widely - outcome measurement challenging but improving

valueDomains.name: Citizen Service Improvement
valueDomains.description: Reducing friction, wait times, and effort for citizens accessing government services.

valueDomains.name: Case / Claims / Application Backlog Reduction
valueDomains.description: Eliminating pending work beyond service standards through automation, workflow optimization, and capacity expansion.

valueDomains.name: Transaction / Determination / Disposition Time Reduction
valueDomains.description: Accelerating individual case or transaction processing through automation, integration, and streamlined workflows.

valueDomains.name: Cost Avoidance
valueDomains.description: Preventing future costs through proactive measures (maintenance, compliance, security) rather than reactive response.
valueDomains.confidence: MEDIUM - avoidance harder to prove than savings

valueDomains.name: Fraud, Waste, and Abuse Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reducing improper payments, detecting fraud, and recovering erroneous payments.
valueDomains.confidence: HIGH - improper payment rates tracked federally

valueDomains.name: Regulatory Compliance Improvement
valueDomains.description: Reducing audit findings, meeting deadlines, and lowering compliance overhead.

valueDomains.name: Transparency and Accountability
valueDomains.description: Enabling public oversight, open data, and performance reporting.
valueDomains.confidence: MEDIUM - value qualitative unless tied to litigation avoidance

valueDomains.name: Grant Utilization and Drawdown Optimization
valueDomains.description: Maximizing federal and state grant fund usage while minimizing lapse.

valueDomains.name: Emergency and Disaster Response Speed
valueDomains.description: Reducing time to deploy resources, establish communications, and restore services.

valueDomains.name: Workforce Productivity and Efficiency
valueDomains.description: Increasing output per FTE, reducing vacancy impact, and lowering contractor dependency.

valueDomains.name: Cybersecurity Risk Reduction
valueDomains.description: Lowering probability and impact of security incidents through controls, monitoring, and Zero Trust.
valueDomains.confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH - incident probability hard to predict; cost of breach better documented

valueDomains.name: Legacy System Cost Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reducing operations and maintenance spend on aging systems through modernization, migration, or replacement.

valueDomains.name: Revenue Collection Improvement
valueDomains.description: Maximizing tax, fee, and revenue capture through improved systems, analytics, and enforcement.

governance.reviewOwner: Master Pack Foundation Architect - Public Sector
